name: Lluc Sanctuary - Pollença Loop Trail
name_original: Santuario de Lluc — circular desde Pollença — Paraje Natural de la Sierra de Tramuntana
description: This difficult 28.4-mile mountain bike route from Pollença to Santuario de Lluc offers challenging terrain and stunning Serra de Tramuntana
region_intro: Mountain biking around Pollença offers varied terrain within the northern foothills of Mallorca's Serra de Tramuntana mountain range. The region features rugged mountains, picturesque coastlines, and lush valleys, providing a diverse environment for mountain bikers. Trails often navigate through the dramatic Serra de Tramuntana, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, characterized by significant elevation changes and technical sections. This landscape provides extensive opportunities for riders of various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Pollença The most popular mountain bike trail is Lluc Sanctuary - Pollença Loop Trail, a 28.4 miles (45.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route showcases the stunning Serra de Tramuntana. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Lluc Sanctuary – Coll de Femenia (515 meters) loop from Port de Pollença, a difficult 35.1 miles (56.5 km) path. This route winds through the dramatic Serra de Tramuntana, offering significant climbs and descents. Local mountain bikers also love the Coll de Femenia (515 meters) – Port of Sa Calobra loop from Pollença, a 53.0 miles (85.3 km) trail leading through the challenging Serra de Tramuntana mountains, often completed in about 6 hours 44 minutes. Mountain biking around Pollença is defined by rugged mountains, picturesque coastlines, and lush valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from technical descents to more accessible routes. The routes in Pollença are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1,200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Pollença's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Pollença?
A: Pollença offers a diverse network of over 40 mountain bike trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 13 easy, 21 moderate, and 9 difficult options, ensuring there's something for every rider.

Q: What is the terrain like for mountain biking in Pollença?
A: The terrain around Pollença is highly varied, ranging from rugged mountains in the Serra de Tramuntana to picturesque coastlines and lush valleys. You can expect significant elevation changes, technical sections, and trails that wind through a UNESCO World Heritage Site, offering breathtaking views.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Pollença?
A: Yes, Pollença has 13 easy mountain bike trails that are suitable for beginners or families looking for a less challenging ride. These routes often traverse valleys and less technical areas, providing a pleasant experience amidst the region's natural beauty.

Q: What challenging routes are available for experienced mountain bikers?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Pollença offers 9 difficult mountain bike trails. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004399767/lluc-sanctuary-pollenca-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lluc Sanctuary - Pollença Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; (45.7 km with over 1000m elevation gain) or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1462592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lluc Sanctuary – Coll de Femenia loop from Port de Pollença&lt;/a&gt; (56.5 km) provide significant climbs and technical descents through the Serra de Tramuntana.

Q: Are there loop mountain bike trails in Pollença?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Pollença are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004399767/lluc-sanctuary-pollenca-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lluc Sanctuary - Pollença Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38140606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Formentor Beach – View of Cap de Formentor loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I expect to see along the trails?
A: Mountain biking in Pollença offers stunning natural features and viewpoints. You'll encounter the dramatic cliffs of Cap de Formentor, the panoramic vistas from Puig de Pollença (Puig de Maria), and the rugged beauty of the Serra de Tramuntana mountains. Many routes also offer incredible sea views along the coastline.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Pollença?
A: The mountain biking routes in Pollença are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse terrain, the breathtaking views of the Serra de Tramuntana and the coastline, and the variety of trails suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, several interesting landmarks and attractions are accessible near the mountain bike routes. You can explore the iconic Formentor Lighthouse, visit the 14th-century monastery at Puig de Pollença, or discover the ancient Roman Bridge. The area also features beautiful beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5709761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Formentor Beach&lt;/a&gt; and natural areas like La Gola Nature Park.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Pollença?
A: While many outdoor areas in Mallorca are dog-friendly, specific regulations can vary by trail and protected area. It's always best to check local signage or official park guidelines for each specific trail to ensure dogs are permitted, especially in nature reserves or private land sections.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Pollença?
A: Pollença offers optimal weather conditions for mountain biking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and less crowded trails. Even winter can be suitable, with mild weather making it an attractive destination when other regions are colder.

Q: Are there any bike parks or dedicated singletracks in the Pollença area?
A: Yes, the Pollença area features several dedicated singletracks and bike park elements. You can find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/311261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonaire Singletrail&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5552541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pine Forest Singletrack Trail&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5633992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Justy's Flow Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which offer technical features and flow sections for mountain bikers.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in Pollença?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Pollença town and Port de Pollença. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check the starting points of individual routes on komoot, as many popular trails have designated parking areas nearby. Some routes, particularly those in the Serra de Tramuntana, might have limited parking, so planning ahead is recommended.
tips: A fun path with very technical rocky areas where you can enjoy the authentic MTB both up and down.

The Santuari de Lluc is located at 525 meters altitude in a basin of the Tramuntana mountains. In addition to its sacred significance, it offers bikers above all a rewarding rest area with very good food. With its accommodations, it is also an idyllic starting point for bike tours in Mallorca. If you're looking for peace, you're right here.
Rocky path with magnificent views of Monte de ses Covasses (753 m.) from the pass.

faqs.question: What level of fitness and skill is required for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult and is designed for experienced mountain bikers. It demands a very high level of physical fitness and advanced riding skills. Be prepared for challenging sections that may even require pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse and demanding, featuring a mix of surfaces. You'll encounter asphalt, unpaved roads, paved sections, gravel, and natural paths. A notable section is the Fun trail of Ses Covasses, which includes very technical rocky areas, offering an authentic mountain biking experience both uphill and downhill.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular scenery and cultural landmarks. Key highlights include the historic Lluc Sanctuary, which serves as a significant cultural and spiritual stop. You'll also experience the challenging Fun trail of Ses Covasses and enjoy breathtaking vistas from the View of Ses Covasses from the Collado de la Rota.

faqs.question: Where does the mountain bike route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route that begins and ends in Pollença. You can typically find parking within Pollença itself, which serves as a convenient starting point for your journey into the Tramuntana mountains.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 45.7 kilometers long with over 1,000 meters of elevation gain. Given its difficulty and technical sections, it typically takes around 4 hours and 5 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sierra de Tramuntana generally allows dogs, this specific route is very long, technically demanding, and covers diverse terrain including some alpine sections. It's not ideal for most dogs due to the distance, difficulty, and potential for sharp rocks. If you do bring a dog, ensure they are extremely fit, well-behaved, and always kept on a leash, especially in protected areas and near livestock.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for mountain biking in the Paraje Natural de la Sierra de Tramuntana?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on established trails within the Paraje Natural de la Sierra de Tramuntana. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of private property. The area is a UNESCO World Heritage site, so responsible behavior is crucial for its preservation.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle this route is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the temperatures are milder, making the considerable physical effort more manageable. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder, wetter conditions or even snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the Santuario de Lluc?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Lluc Sanctuary is a significant hub and offers amenities. You'll find options for food and accommodation there, making it an excellent place to rest and refuel during or after your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the GR 221 Dry Stone Route: Stage 7: Son Amer - Pont Romà, El Camí IT4TR3 El Camí Illenc: de l'Alguer a Formentera, and the camí Vell Pollença-Lluc (GR-221), among others.

faqs.question: What type of bike is recommended for this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the demanding and technical nature of the terrain, including rocky sections and significant elevation changes, a full-suspension mountain bike is highly recommended for comfort and control. An eMTB could also be a good option to assist with the considerable climbs and overall distance.

waypoints.name: View of Ses Covasses from the Collado de la Rota
